name: Active Directory Replication User Backdoor (DCSync Permissions)
description: Detects a potential Active Directory backdoor where the `nTSecurityDescriptor` attribute of a domain object is modified to grant Active Directory replication permissions to a user or computer account. This modification enables the granted account to perform DCSync attacks, allowing an adversary to extract credential hashes for all domain users.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the Domain Controller **{computer_name}**: the **`nTSecurityDescriptor`** attribute on the Active Directory object **{object_dn}** was modified by the user **{subject_user_name}**. The change includes the addition of one or more of the following replication permissions: `Replicating Directory Changes` or `Replicating Directory Changes All`, indicated by the GUID(s) in the `AttributeValue` of **{attribute_value}**. This highly suspicious activity is a known technique for creating a **DCSync backdoor** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1003.003**), which allows an attacker to steal all domain user credentials. Immediate action is required to investigate the legitimacy of this change and to revoke the permissions. This is a high-confidence alert for a severe security incident and should be treated as a confirmed compromise until proven otherwise.

parameters.name: subject_user_name
parameters.description: The user account that initiated the modification.

parameters.name: object_dn
parameters.description: The distinguished name of the Active Directory object whose permissions were modified.

parameters.name: attribute_value
parameters.description: The value of the `nTSecurityDescriptor` attribute, containing the GUIDs of the replication rights.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The hostname of the Domain Controller where the event occurred.
